purpose: Large-scale KRITIS hardening program for Ananta with immediate focus on auditability and write approvals, followed by safety/stability controls and infrastructure-grade isolation and RBAC.
goal: Ananta should become suitable for higher-trust and KRITIS-like environments by enforcing auditable LLM interactions, explicit write approvals, secure retrieval filtering, deterministic workflow control, hardened execution isolation and enterprise-grade role handling.
program_success_criteria: Every security-relevant LLM and tool interaction can be reconstructed through an auditable chain.
No code or file mutation path can bypass an explicit approval boundary where required.
RAG and context assembly can respect metadata-based security boundaries.
Workflow behavior is constrained enough to avoid unsafe hidden transitions.
Execution paths run in hardened isolated environments suitable for higher-risk usage.
Enterprise users and admins can operate the system with clear role and permission boundaries in both backend and UI.

groups.title: KRITIS Prioritaet 1 - Audit-Logging Middleware
groups.goal: Develop a complete audit layer for all relevant LLM, tool, context, approval and mutation interactions.
success_criteria: All relevant LLM and tool interactions are audit-linked through traceable records.
The audit chain remains useful even with redaction enabled.
Operators can reconstruct who did what, with which context class, under which policy and with which result.

tasks.title: Audit coverage target model define
acceptance_criteria: A target model defines which operations must always be audited.
The model covers LLM generation, tool calls, approvals, writes, workflow transitions, repair actions and high-risk reads.
The model distinguishes mandatory, optional and forbidden audit payload classes.

tasks.title: Canonical audit event schema define
acceptance_criteria: A single schema exists for audit events across routes and services.
The schema includes at least trace_id, task_id, actor, role, policy_version, operation_type, target, outcome and timestamps.
The schema supports correlated multi-step chains.

tasks.title: Audit logging middleware layer implement
acceptance_criteria: A middleware or central hook layer emits audit records for relevant request and execution flows.
The implementation avoids route-by-route duplication where possible.
Coverage extends to synchronous and asynchronous critical paths.

tasks.title: LLM interaction audit hooks add
acceptance_criteria: Every LLM interaction emits auditable metadata.
The audit record includes provider/backend, model, prompt bundle class, context budget summary and response outcome.
Raw prompt storage is governed separately from metadata logging.

tasks.title: Prompt and context class logging design add
acceptance_criteria: Audit records can express what kind of context was sent without blindly storing sensitive content.
Context classes distinguish repo, memory, artifact, wiki, external research and user input.
The design is useful for later KRITIS evidence and incident reconstruction.

tasks.title: Tool call audit chaining implement
acceptance_criteria: Tool calls are linked to the LLM interaction that initiated them.
Audit records show approval state, execution outcome and affected target scope.
Repeated or nested tool calls remain traceable.

tasks.title: Write operation audit detail add
acceptance_criteria: Every file/code/artifact mutation emits structured audit detail.
Audit output includes target path class, write reason, approval source and verification result.
High-risk writes are distinguishable from low-risk writes.

tasks.title: Approval event audit integration add
acceptance_criteria: Approvals, rejections and expired approvals are fully audited.
The records identify approver identity and approval scope.
Audit records can reconstruct whether a write happened with or without valid approval.

tasks.title: Workflow transition audit events add
acceptance_criteria: Critical workflow state transitions emit structured audit records.
The records include from_state, to_state, trigger and policy context.
Unexpected transitions are especially visible.

tasks.title: Audit redaction rules harden
acceptance_criteria: Audit logs respect central redaction policies.
Sensitive secrets and credentials are never stored in plain form at normal visibility levels.
The result remains forensically useful without leaking sensitive data.

tasks.title: Audit retention and storage policy define
acceptance_criteria: Retention periods and storage classes are defined for audit data.
The policy distinguishes operational short-term logs from long-term evidence.
Rotation and archival behavior are documented.

tasks.title: Audit integrity and tamper evidence evaluate
acceptance_criteria: A strategy exists for tamper-evident audit storage or export.
The design includes hash chaining, signed export or another integrity method.
The approach is realistic for Ananta operations.

tasks.title: Audit query surface improve
acceptance_criteria: Operators can query audit records by task, trace, actor, date and event class.
The audit view supports practical incident analysis.
The query surface respects RBAC and redaction.

tasks.title: Audit read-model summary add
acceptance_criteria: Dashboard or assistant summaries can indicate high-level audit health and recent critical events.
The summary remains compact and useful.
Detailed raw audit review stays separate.

tasks.title: Audit contract tests add
acceptance_criteria: Core audit schemas and event emissions are protected by tests.
Tests confirm that high-risk flows cannot silently skip audit generation.
Regression coverage exists for LLM, tool, approval and write paths.

tasks.title: Audit smoke checklist for KRITIS evidence add
acceptance_criteria: A manual checklist verifies audit completeness for representative critical flows.
The checklist is suitable for release evidence gathering.
The checklist includes negative and blocked operation cases.

groups.title: KRITIS Prioritaet 1 - Evolver Write-Lock
groups.goal: Require explicit backend approval before any Evolver-driven or related code mutation can be written.
success_criteria: No Evolver-related write path can bypass approval.
Approval scope is explicit, time-bounded and auditable.
Apply behavior remains fail-closed unless approval and policy requirements are satisfied.

tasks.title: Write-lock target model define
acceptance_criteria: A target model defines what counts as a write-locked operation.
The model covers file writes, patch application, generated code insertion and mutation-like artifact application.
The model clearly separates analyze-only from mutation-capable steps.

tasks.title: Approval boundary for Evolver implement
acceptance_criteria: Evolver write paths call a single approval boundary before mutation.
No hidden fallback path can write without crossing the boundary.
The boundary is backend-enforced and not UI-only.

tasks.title: Approval scope model define
acceptance_criteria: Approval can be bound to target files, operation class, time window and trace.
Approvals cannot be reused broadly outside their intended scope.
Expired or mismatched approvals fail closed.

tasks.title: Pre-write verification gate add
acceptance_criteria: Before a write is executed, the system verifies approval, policy class and target scope.
The verification gate can reject unsafe apply attempts.
Rejections are visible and auditable.

tasks.title: Apply token or approval artifact design add
acceptance_criteria: An explicit approval artifact or token model is defined.
The approval object is linked to audit events and write operations.
The model prevents ambiguous 'approved somewhere earlier' behavior.

tasks.title: Write target normalization add
acceptance_criteria: Write targets are normalized before approval checking.
Normalization avoids path tricks and equivalent-path bypasses.
The target model is compatible with future sandboxing.

tasks.title: Review-first approval UX/API path add
acceptance_criteria: Approvals can be issued only after reviewing structured proposal details.
The API/UI path does not encourage blind mass approval.
The review path shows affected files and operation class.

tasks.title: Emergency deny switch for mutation paths add
acceptance_criteria: Operators can globally disable mutation-capable Evolver paths quickly.
The kill switch is visible in configuration and diagnostics.
The switch fails closed.

tasks.title: Approval and write-lock contract tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove that write-capable Evolver paths cannot bypass approval.
Tests cover expired approval, wrong target and no-approval cases.
Tests cover at least one successful approved write path.

tasks.title: Evolver mutation evidence checklist add
acceptance_criteria: A checklist exists for validating mutation gating in release or audit reviews.
The checklist references audit evidence, approval evidence and write evidence.
The checklist is suitable for KRITIS reviews.

groups.title: KRITIS Prioritaet 2 - RAG Metadata-Filtering
groups.goal: Extend retrieval and context assembly with metadata-based security tags and filtering rules.
success_criteria: Documents and chunks can carry security metadata.
Retrieval respects classification and policy boundaries.
Context assembly does not silently mix sources across forbidden security domains.

tasks.title: Security metadata model for retrieval define
acceptance_criteria: A metadata model exists for documents, chunks and retrieval documents.
The model supports classification, source origin, sensitivity, tenancy and approval class.
The model is usable across repo, artifact, wiki and external research sources.

tasks.title: Chunk-level security tagging add
acceptance_criteria: The indexing pipeline can attach security tags at chunk level where needed.
Chunk tags can differ from file-level tags if finer boundaries are necessary.
The tagging design stays practical.

tasks.title: Retrieval policy filter service implement
acceptance_criteria: Retrieval passes through a policy filter before results reach prompt bundles.
The filter can allow, deny or downgrade items based on metadata.
The filter is reusable across retrieval entry points.

tasks.title: Context source segregation rules define
acceptance_criteria: The system defines when source classes may be mixed and when they must be isolated.
Rules cover repo code, task memory, internal docs, offline wiki and external research.
Mixing policy is explicit and testable.

tasks.title: Default deny behavior for unknown sensitivity add
acceptance_criteria: Unknown or missing security metadata does not default to broad inclusion in high-trust modes.
The fallback is documented and fail-closed where appropriate.
Operators can see when unknown metadata caused exclusion.

tasks.title: Metadata propagation through compaction and memory add
acceptance_criteria: Compacted summaries and retrieval documents retain or derive safe security metadata.
Security properties are not lost when context is compacted.
Memory entries remain filterable later.

tasks.title: RAG diagnostics for filtered context add
acceptance_criteria: Operators can inspect why retrieval results were filtered or downgraded.
Diagnostics remain safe and do not leak forbidden content.
The information is sufficient for policy debugging.

tasks.title: RAG metadata admin tooling add
acceptance_criteria: There is an admin path for tagging or retagging indexed content where needed.
Batch operations are possible for common cases.
The tooling remains auditable.

tasks.title: Contract and regression tests for metadata filtering add
acceptance_criteria: Tests prove that forbidden content is excluded from prompt bundles under the relevant policy.
Tests cover allowed, denied and unknown-metadata cases.
Mixed-source retrieval behavior is covered.

tasks.title: Security-focused retrieval benchmark add
acceptance_criteria: A small benchmark exists for security tagging and retrieval correctness.
The benchmark checks both relevance and isolation behavior.
The benchmark is reusable in release checks.

groups.title: KRITIS Prioritaet 2 - Workflow Deterministik
groups.goal: Introduce stricter state transitions and bounded workflow behavior for critical agent flows.
success_criteria: Critical flows use explicit state models.
Fallbacks and implicit jumps are reduced or made explicit.

tasks.title: Deterministic workflow target model define
acceptance_criteria: A target model defines deterministic expectations for critical Ananta workflows.
The model distinguishes flexible user-facing flows from safety-critical state machines.
The model identifies flows that require strict control first.

tasks.title: Critical flow inventory create
acceptance_criteria: An inventory lists all critical flows such as approval, mutation, repair, review and high-risk execution.
Each flow has named states and intended transitions.
The inventory becomes the basis for enforcement and tests.

tasks.title: Explicit state machine for critical workflows add
acceptance_criteria: At least the most critical workflows gain explicit state machine enforcement.
Transitions are validated instead of inferred ad hoc.
Invalid transitions fail with structured errors.

tasks.title: Transition guard layer implement
acceptance_criteria: A transition guard layer checks policy, approval and preconditions before state changes.
The layer is reusable across critical workflow types.
Transition denials are auditable.

tasks.title: Fallback behavior explicitness improve
acceptance_criteria: Fallbacks are visible and structured in stateful critical flows.
Silent rerouting or mutation-capable fallback behavior is removed where unsafe.
The system can explain why a fallback happened.

tasks.title: Replayability and trace reconstruction improve
acceptance_criteria: A critical workflow can be reconstructed from state records and audit traces.
Replay or simulation for incident analysis is practical enough for operators.
The implementation does not depend on raw unstructured logs alone.

tasks.title: Timeout and stuck-state handling add
acceptance_criteria: Critical workflows handle timeout, waiting and interrupted states explicitly.
Stuck states are detectable and recoverable.
Recovery paths are bounded and auditable.

tasks.title: Deterministic workflow tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover valid and invalid transitions for critical flows.
Mutation and approval flows have especially strong state coverage.
Regression tests prevent accidental loosening of constraints.

tasks.title: State transition observability add
acceptance_criteria: Metrics or summaries show transition failures, blocked transitions and unusual patterns.
Operators can detect unstable workflow behavior quickly.
The signals remain interpretable.

groups.title: KRITIS Prioritaet 3 - Execution Sandboxing
groups.goal: Move execution and test runtime environments toward hardened container isolation suitable for higher-risk operation.
success_criteria: Execution paths are more strongly isolated than today.
High-risk commands and tools run in constrained environments.
Isolation boundaries are documented, tested and auditable.

tasks.title: Sandbox threat model define
acceptance_criteria: A threat model exists for execution and test environments.
The model covers file access, network access, privilege escalation, container breakout and host impact.
The model distinguishes dev convenience from KRITIS mode.

tasks.title: Execution isolation classes define
acceptance_criteria: The system defines at least a few isolation classes such as low-risk, bounded mutable and hardened high-risk execution.
Task kinds or tools can map to these classes.
The classes are enforceable.

tasks.title: Hardened container profile create
acceptance_criteria: A hardened execution profile exists for containerized runs.
The profile addresses user privileges, filesystem mutability, capability drops and runtime constraints.
The profile is documented and testable.

tasks.title: Filesystem boundary controls add
acceptance_criteria: Writable locations are explicit and minimal.
Sensitive host paths and unrelated project areas are not writable by default.
The model supports future write-lock policy integration.

tasks.title: Network egress control strategy define
acceptance_criteria: A strategy exists for controlling outbound network access in hardened execution contexts.
The policy distinguishes no-network, allowlisted network and broad network cases.
The approach is realistic for Ananta deployment modes.

tasks.title: Command execution wrappers enforce sandbox classes
acceptance_criteria: Execution wrappers can choose the required isolation class per action.
High-risk actions cannot silently run in weak isolation mode in hardened profiles.
Isolation choice is auditable.

tasks.title: Terminal access restrictions harden
acceptance_criteria: Interactive terminal behavior is reviewed against hardened mode requirements.
Read-only and interactive modes remain clearly separated.
Additional controls are defined for KRITIS-style use.

tasks.title: Sandboxed test environment rollout define
acceptance_criteria: The test/lab execution environment has a rollout path toward hardened isolation.
The rollout includes compatibility and failure recovery considerations.
The path does not break all existing developer workflows at once.

tasks.title: Sandbox escape and policy regression tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify key isolation assumptions for critical sandbox classes.
The suite includes representative denied actions.
Regression tests protect hardened profiles from silent weakening.

tasks.title: Sandbox operations guide add
acceptance_criteria: Operators have a practical guide for running Ananta in hardened execution mode.
The guide covers tradeoffs, limitations and known compatibility issues.
The guide is suitable for higher-trust environments.

groups.title: KRITIS Prioritaet 3 - Enterprise RBAC
groups.goal: Implement clear enterprise-grade role and permission handling in the Angular interface aligned with backend authorization.
success_criteria: The UI reflects backend role boundaries consistently.
Sensitive views and actions respect role-based access rules.
Admins can operate user and role management through a usable interface.

tasks.title: RBAC model alignment review perform
acceptance_criteria: A review aligns backend auth model, policy model and frontend visibility rules.
The review identifies missing UI enforcement and missing role semantics.
The result defines canonical role names and action classes.

tasks.title: Frontend permission matrix define
acceptance_criteria: A permission matrix exists for views, actions and admin operations in Angular.
The matrix includes at least user, admin and any planned enterprise operator roles.
The matrix is specific enough to drive implementation.

tasks.title: Frontend guard and action-policy layer implement
acceptance_criteria: Angular routes and action surfaces use a shared permission layer.
UI access decisions are not scattered ad hoc across components.
Denied states are handled gracefully.

tasks.title: User and role management UI implement
acceptance_criteria: Admins can view users and manage roles through the UI.
The interface is usable enough for real administration.
Critical role changes remain explicit and auditable.

tasks.title: Sensitive action gating in UI add
acceptance_criteria: High-risk actions such as approvals, write applies, terminal access and policy changes are hidden or disabled appropriately.
The UI reflects pending approval versus permission denial clearly.
The UI never implies permission where the backend would deny.

tasks.title: Admin read-model and diagnostics views refine
acceptance_criteria: Admin-only diagnostics and policy summaries have dedicated UI handling.
Lower-privilege users do not see irrelevant or sensitive admin details.
Admin views remain practical rather than purely technical dumps.

tasks.title: Role change audit visibility add
acceptance_criteria: UI users with the right role can inspect recent role and permission changes.
The view is linked to audit evidence where possible.
The feature improves governance transparency.

tasks.title: Frontend RBAC tests add
acceptance_criteria: Tests verify route guards, action visibility and permission handling.
Representative admin and non-admin flows are covered.
The tests reduce risk of accidental UI exposure.

tasks.title: End-to-end authorization consistency tests add
acceptance_criteria: E2E tests confirm UI behavior matches backend authorization outcomes.
The tests cover at least a few sensitive actions and views.
Mismatch cases are caught before release.

tasks.title: Enterprise admin operations guide add
acceptance_criteria: A concise guide explains user, role and permission administration in the UI.
The guide fits real enterprise or KRITIS operational use.
The guide includes common safety warnings.

cross_cutting_tasks.title: KRITIS profile and rollout strategy define
acceptance_criteria: A dedicated KRITIS-oriented profile or mode is defined.
The profile binds together audit, write-lock, retrieval filtering, deterministic workflows, sandboxing and RBAC expectations.
The rollout path from normal operation to KRITIS-oriented operation is documented.

cross_cutting_tasks.title: Integrated evidence register for KRITIS hardening add
acceptance_criteria: Evidence artifacts across all KRITIS groups are collected in one structured place.
The register is suitable for internal review and release acceptance.
The register links tests, audit evidence, policy evidence and operational guidance.

cross_cutting_tasks.title: Program-level benchmark and acceptance suite define
acceptance_criteria: A program-level validation suite exists for the KRITIS hardening effort.
The suite covers at least one representative flow from each priority group.
The suite is reusable for later regressions and releases.

cross_cutting_tasks.title: Program-level release gate define
acceptance_criteria: The project defines what must be true before claiming KRITIS-grade hardening for a release.
Mandatory gates are explicit and test-linked.
The release gate avoids vague partial compliance claims.
